source.description: One reviewed six-panel handmade marker contact sheet cropped into five cumulative process masters and one final superhero-mask master.
source.notes: Keep the same face-free front-facing mask, swept brow silhouette, center nose dip, two eye openings, side tie ribbons, purple fill, and cyan accents in every panel.

process_strategy: One contact sheet builds a face-free superhero mask from light symmetry guides through its outer contour, eye openings, and tie ribbons before inking and coloring only established shapes; the final panel only strengthens lines and highlights.

final_elements.name: symmetry guides
final_elements.description: A light horizontal eye axis, vertical center line, and two shallow guide arcs establishing the mask's width and tilt.

final_elements.name: outer mask silhouette
final_elements.description: A swept upper brow contour, pointed outer corners, rounded cheek edges, and a small center nose dip.

final_elements.name: eye openings
final_elements.description: Two matching almond-shaped eye openings positioned on the established eye axis.

final_elements.name: side tie ribbons and crease marks
final_elements.description: Short ribbon tails at both outer corners plus two small contour creases above the eyes.

final_elements.name: purple fill and cyan accents
final_elements.description: Purple marker across the established mask with cyan accents around the existing eye openings and tie tips.

frames.visible_job: Draw a light horizontal eye axis, vertical center line, and shallow upper and lower guide arcs.
Use the guides to draw the full outer mask silhouette with pointed corners and a center nose dip.
Add two matching almond-shaped eye openings along the established eye axis.
Attach short ribbon tails to both outer corners and add two small brow crease marks.
introduces: side tie ribbons and crease marks

frames.visible_job: Trace the established mask, openings, ribbons, and creases in black, then fill the existing mask purple and its eye rims and tie tips cyan.
requires_prior_elements: side tie ribbons and crease marks

final_step.visible_job: Only erase the remaining light construction guides, strengthen existing black contours, tidy the purple and cyan marker fills, and add tiny white highlights to already-colored areas.

rejection_checks: Reject if the mask width, brow sweep, nose dip, eye spacing, or ribbon placement changes between panels.
Reject if an outline or color pass is the first appearance of a mask contour, eye opening, ribbon, or crease.
Reject clean vector-icon edges, polished poster rendering, eyes, a face, words, logos, sticker outlines, badge frames, labels, or decorative borders.
